title: Timing, Resource Graphs, Transfer, Caching, and User-Performance Boundaries · Language Clinic: User Performance Boundary
summary: The audit calls server response time page speed, double-counts cached resources, ignores compression and render work, and reports one lab run as user experience. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the user performance boundary increment in bounded page-resource and timing audit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit the…
objectives: Separate crawler timings, controlled browser lab evidence, field data, accessibility, network profile, device profile, and user experience claims.
Inspect Cache-Control, Age, validators, Vary, content identity, and browser observations without predicting every intermediary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-performance-resource-audit-3
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-performance-resource-audit-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: the target owner must be able to pause the crawl.
# Changed case: change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance.
def worked_scraper_performance_resource_audit_3(pages: int = 12, resources: int = 180, transfer_bytes: int = 2400000, expanded_bytes: int = 6700000, user_experience_claims: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorizationworked3",
        "url5798",
        "response3dekrow",
        "treeworked35798",
        "frontier5798worked3",
        "report3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or resources < pages:
        return False, resources, "resource-count"
    if transfer_bytes < 0 or expanded_bytes < transfer_bytes:
        return False, expanded_bytes, "transfer-accounting"
    if user_experience_claims > 0:
        return False, user_experience_claims, "user-performance-overclaim"
    if resources > 1000:
        return False, resources, "resource-budget"
    assert expanded_bytes >= transfer_bytes and user_experience_claims == 0
    return True, resources, "bounded-resource-audit"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Build User Performance Boundary evidence · Theory User Performance B…
steps.instruction:

Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-user-performance-boundary-1xmuwq
# Competency: scraper-user-performance-boundary.
# Case 9834: a multilingual public-service site review.
# Operating constraint: no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def evidence_scraper_user_performance_boundary_1xmuwq(pages: int = 12, resources: int = 180, transfer_bytes: int = 2400000, expanded_bytes: int = 6700000, user_experience_claims: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1xmuwq = "1xmuwq-qwumx1-9834"
    assert evidence_variant_1xmuwq
    evidence_axes_1xmuwq = (
        "authorization1xmuwq",
        "url9834",
        "responseqwumx1",
        "tree1xmuwq9834",
        "frontier98341xmuwq",
        "reportqwumx19834",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1xmuwq)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or resources < pages:
        return False, resources, "resource-count"
    if transfer_bytes < 0 or expanded_bytes < transfer_bytes:
        return False, expanded_bytes, "transfer-accounting"
    if user_experience_claims > 0:
        return False, user_experience_claims, "user-performance-overclaim"
    if resources > 1000:
        return False, resources, "resource-budget"
    assert expanded_bytes >= transfer_bytes and user_experience_claims == 0
    return True, resources, "bounded-resource-audit"
